title: Mildly Sweet and Crunchy Mexican Christmas Pudding Mole Poblano
description: A unique and refreshing twist on traditional Mexican cuisine, combining the sweetness of Christmas pudding with the crunch of a fresh, sashimi-cut accompaniment, in a delicate pandan leaf wrap, inspired by the simple flavors of a Buddhist temple meal.
instructions: Combine Christmas pudding and cocoa powder in a mixing bowl, breaking up any lumps.
Add cinnamon and anise powders, and mix until well combined.
Heat a dry pan over low heat, and toast the pandan leaves until fragrant.
Roll each pandan leaf into a tight cylinder, and fill with the Christmas pudding mixture, leaving a small border at the top.
Fold the pandan leaf over the filling to create a neat, compact package.
Repeat with the remaining pandan leaves and filling, to make 12 individual servings.
Serve immediately, garnished with toasted sesame seeds or chopped nuts, if desired.
To toast sesame seeds or nuts, heat a small pan over low heat, and cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
To make the mole poblano, combine the toasted pandan leaves, sesame seeds, and nuts in a blender or food processor, and blend until well combined.
Add a small amount of water to the blender, and blend until the mixture reaches a smooth, thick paste.
Strain the paste through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding any solids.
Refrigerate the mole poblano until chilled, before serving.
Use the mole poblano to garnish the Christmas pudding pudding, or serve it on the side for guests to add as desired.
